title: Mattress assembly
abstract: A mattress assembly (10) is provided, comprising a core layer (11) composed of a first foam material (12) and a surface layer formed of a second foam material (21). The core layer (11) comprises at least two rectangular-shaped cavities (13A, 13B) formed by vertical blind-cuts to a predetermined depth across the width of the core layer, each said cavity (13A, 13B) receives an at least one array of pocket spring units (14A, 14B). The spring units (14 A, 14B) form regions ii, iv with support parameters distinct from the support parameters of the first foam material, in terms of at least firmness and resilience. Spring unit regions ii, iv together with the first foam material regions i, iii, v constitute a localized pressure point relief system within the mattress assembly (10).
claims: Claims Claims 1. A mattress assembly (10) comprising a core layer (11) composed of a first foam material (12) and comprising at least two rectangular-shaped cavities (13 A, 13B) formed by vertical blind-cuts to a predetermined depth across the width of the core layer, each said cavity (13 A, 13B) configured to receive a plurality of pocket spring units (14A, 14B) arranged into an at least one array, wherein the core layer (11) encompasses at least five regions (i, ii, iii, iv, v) transversely adjacent to each other such, that the regions (ii) and (iv), which comprise the cavities (13 A, 13B) occupied by the pocket spring units (14A, 14B), alternate along the length of the core layer (11) with the regions (i), (iii) and (v) formed by the first foam material, said regions (ii) and (iv) being distinct from the regions (i), (iii) and (v) in terms of at least firmness and resilience. 2. The mattress assembly (10) of claim 1, wherein the regions (ii) and (iv) comprising the cavities (13 A, 13B) occupied by the pocket spring units (14A, 14B) are configured to provide softer support to a user of the mattress assembly, as compared to the regions (i), (iii) and (v) formed by the first foam material (12). 3. The mattress assembly (10) of any one of claims 1 or 2, wherein the regions (ii) and (iv) correspond to at least shoulder and hip regions of the human body. 4. The mattress assembly (10) of any one of the preceding claim, wherein resilience rate in the regions (i), (iii) and (v) formed by the first foam material (12) exceeds resilience rate in the regions (ii) and (iv) comprising the cavities (13 A, 13B) occupied by the pocket spring units (14A, 14B). 5. The mattress assembly (10) of any one of the preceding claim, wherein each pocket spring unit (14A, 14B) comprises a coil spring individually packed into a fabric envelope. 6. The mattress assembly (10) of any one of the preceding claim, wherein the pocket spring units (14A, 14B) are 25 - 45 mm high, preferably 30 - 40 mm high. 7. The mattress assembly (10) of any one of the preceding claim, comprising within each cavity (13 A, 13B) two overlaying arrays of the pocket spring units (14A, 14B). 8. The mattress assembly (10) of any one of the preceding claim, further comprising a plurality of vertical slots (15 A, 15B) arranged into parallel rows at the bottom of each cavity (13 A, 13B), wherein each individual slot (15 A, 15B) is a vertical blind-cut extending from the bottom of the cavity (13 A, 13B) to a predetermined depth in the core layer (11) across the width of said core layer (11). 9. The mattress assembly (10) of any one of the preceding claim, wherein the regions (ii) and (iv) comprising the cavities (13 A, 13B) occupied by the pocket spring units (14A, 14B) and the regions (i), (iii) and (v) formed by the first foam material (12), constitute a localized pressure point relief system within the core layer (11). 10. The mattress assembly (10) of any one of the preceding claim, wherein density of the first foam material (12) is within a range of 25 - 40 kilograms per cubic meter. 11. The mattress assembly (10) of any one of the preceding claim, further comprising a surface layer (21) composed of a second foam material and disposed on the top of the core layer (11) such, that at least one array of the pocket spring units (14A, 14B) within each cavity (13A, 13B) adjoins said surface layer (21). 12. The mattress assembly (10) of claim 11, wherein the second foam material is an open-cell high-resilience foam configured to dissipate body heat and having density at least 40 kilograms per cubic meter. 13. The mattress assembly (10) of any one of the preceding claim, wherein the first foam material and/or the second foam material are polyurethane foams. 14. The mattress assembly (10) of any one of the preceding claim, further comprising an encasement.
